meta.title: OH&S Policy — {organisation}
meta.subtitle: ISO 45001:2018 clause 5.2 · top-management-signed · context-fit to {organisation}'s significant risks
meta.author: {top-management signatory role/title}
meta.deid_notice: This policy names the organisation and the top-management signatory by role/title only — no individual's personal or health data appears. Any identifier in the supplied context was scrubbed to a role/title label before drafting; no re-identification key appears. Decision-support; a competent person must review this policy before it is adopted.

sections.text: The top-management-signed Occupational Health & Safety policy for {organisation} ({scale — headcount, sites}), issued under ISO 45001:2018 clause 5.2. It is appropriate to the organisation's purpose, size and context and the specific nature of its significant OH&S risks ({named risks}), and is documented, communicated within the organisation, and available to interested parties.
This policy is appropriate to {organisation}'s purpose, size and context. Each commitment below is tied to the organisation's actual significant risks — it is not a generic template.

rows: {how objectives are derived for the named risks}
{the org's applicable legal duties + other requirements}
3 — Eliminate hazards and reduce OH&S risks (hierarchy of controls)
{higher-order controls for the named risks — never PPE-only}
4 — Continual improvement of the OH&S management system
{how workers and their representatives are consulted}
sections.caption: All five mandatory clause-5.2 commitments — each context-fit to {organisation}'s actual risks. A missing commitment is a citation-accuracy hard-fail.

sections.text: Hazard elimination & risk reduction (hierarchy of controls)

Commitment 3 is expressed up the full hierarchy of controls (KB-SNIP-HOC) — eliminate to substitute to engineer to administrate to PPE. The policy never commits the organisation to a PPE-only or administrative-only treatment of a significant risk.

Top management demonstrates leadership and commitment to this policy. Signed by {top-management signatory role/title} on behalf of {organisation}. The policy is reviewed {review cadence} and on significant change.

Where the law compels a written health-and-safety policy, that duty is noted here as a branch beside this policy. For India, the statutory written-policy position defers to hse-india (state detection first) — no national form number is minted; an unverified position is flagged [GAP].

Decision-support only. This policy is a draft for {organisation}'s own competent person to review before adoption; it is not a final legal document. India statutory duty: defer to hse-india (state first); no national-form number minted.

items: ISO 45001:2018 clause 5.2 — OH&S policy (the five mandatory commitments + the appropriate-to-context characteristics).
Statutory written-policy duty by jurisdiction (e.g. UK HSWA 1974 s.2(3) at 5+ employees); India defers to hse-india.
